confirmed.title: Unknown-time honesty (FR-015) depends 100% on FuFirE provisional_fields — no local timeKnown backstop; 12:00 ascendant/houses/hour can be shown as fact
confirmed.featureArea: Unknown birth time mode (FR-015/B-004) / anti-reification honesty
confirmed.evidence: Line 229 reads `const timeKnown = input.timeKnown !== false` but it is ONLY passed through to output (line 768) — never used to drive suppression. Suppression flags come solely from the engine response: `ascendantProvisional = westernProvisionalFields.includes('ascendant')` (232), `housesProvisional` (233), `hourProvisional` (440), where westernProvisionalFields = `raw.western?.precision?.provisional_fields` (230). At line 322-329 the comment admits 'The engine may return angles.Ascendant with a 12:00-computed value' and the else-branch DOES use `angles.Ascendant` whenever `ascendantProvisional` is false. So if a user submits timeKnown:false (payload sends birth_time_known:false + birthTime '12:00' per fufirePayloadMappers.ts:119-124) but FuFirE's response omits/renames precision.provisional_fields (engine version skew, an endpoint that ignores birth_time_known, or any fallback-local path), the app renders a 12:00-derived ascendant, 12 houses and hour pillar AS FACT. fufireNormalizer.test.ts:88-107 only ever exercises pre-populated `unknownWestern/unknownBazi/unknownFusion` fixtures that already contain provisional_fields — there is NO test where timeKnown:false is paired with a raw response lacking provisional_fields. This is the exact anti-reification violation the wording-honesty tests are meant to prevent, but it is unguarded.

confirmed.title: Expensive compute routes are unauthenticated AND unthrottled — unlimited Gemini/FuFirE cost amplification
confirmed.featureArea: BFF / Gemini reading / synastry / daily compute
confirmed.evidence: createApp() (app.ts:260-262) installs only express.json() — no rate-limit, no auth on compute routes. /api/gemini/reading calls the paid Gemini API with GEMINI_API_KEY (app.ts:507-516). /api/azodiac/synastry fires 2 upstream FuFirE /chart calls per request (Promise.all at app.ts:456); /api/azodiac/daily fires bootstrap+daily (2 upstream calls, app.ts:422-434); /api/azodiac/profile orchestrates up to 5 upstream calls (profileService.ts:61-77). All four accept anonymous POSTs with no throttle. package.json dependencies list no rate-limiting package. A scripted client can drive unbounded billable spend (Gemini tokens + FuFirE quota) and saturate the BFF.

confirmed.title: Service-role key bypasses RLS for ALL BFF queries — tenant isolation depends entirely on never forgetting the manual .eq('user_id') filter
confirmed.location: src/server/supabase.ts:13-21 (service-role client) + every handler in app.ts:527-617; RLS policies migration :27-38
confirmed.evidence: getServerSupabase() builds the client with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (supabase.ts:16), which bypasses RLS by design (acknowledged in migration comment :3 and docs/contracts/supabase-schema.md:38). Therefore the RLS policies (migration :30-38) provide NO protection on the actual data path — they are dead defense-in-depth for the BFF. Cross-tenant isolation rests 100% on each handler manually appending `.eq('user_id', req.userId)`. Today every handler does (verified :532,:551,:570,:580,:600,:614) and user_id is always from the verified JWT, never the body — so it is currently CORRECT. The risk is structural: one future query that omits the filter (e.g. a new admin/list/join route, or a forgotten .eq on an UPDATE) leaks or mutates other users' rows with zero DB backstop, and no test would catch it because tests mock the supabase client (app.profiles.test.ts:58-61) rather than exercise real RLS. The makeDefault clear-UPDATE (app.ts:548-551) is the closest call — it relies solely on .eq('user_id') with no RLS net.

confirmed.title: Promise.all in buildProfile fails the whole profile when ONE orchestrated section errors — defeats the normalizer's per-section degradation
confirmed.location: src/utils/profileService.ts:77 (await Promise.all(jobs)); failure surfaces at src/server/app.ts:84-93 resolveProfile
confirmed.evidence: buildProfile pushes up to 4 independent calls (postWestern/postBazi/postWuxing/postFusion) into `jobs` with no per-job .catch, then `await Promise.all(jobs)`. Promise.all rejects on the FIRST rejection, so if e.g. only postFusion returns 503/429/timeout (FuFirEError fufire_unavailable/rate_limited) while western+bazi+wuxing all return 200, the entire await throws and the whole /api/azodiac/profile request 502s. resolveProfile (app.ts:88) only converts to local fallback for config gaps (missing_fufire_url/key) — a transient upstream section failure is rethrown. The normalizer is explicitly engineered to degrade per-section (fufireNormalizer.test 'tolerates a completely empty raw object' / 'degrades per-section instead of throwing'), but that capability is unreachable because the data never reaches it. Promise.allSettled + assigning only fulfilled sections would let 3/4 render with honest missing-state on the 4th.

confirmed.title: Local fallback fabricates retrograde flags and planet longitudes from non-ephemeris formulas, presented as real positions
confirmed.evidence: Outer planets use invented linear mean-longitude models, e.g. `marsLong = sunLong - 18 + 0.524*daysSinceJ2000`, `jupLong = 34.35 + 0.083*days`, with no ephemeris/perturbation terms — accumulating tens of degrees of error per year. retrograde is `(Math.sin(daysSinceJ2000/100 + idx) < -0.6)` — a sine of an index, i.e. a fabricated boolean unrelated to actual planetary motion. These flow to the UI as `retrograde`, `degree`, `house` via fufireNormalizer.ts:812 getRawSimulatedProfileFromLocal → real reading fallback. This is exactly the ANTI-REIFICATION violation the product principle forbids: a fabricated metric shown as a measured fact.

confirmed.title: Ascendant and houses derived from a 15°/hour-from-6AM approximation that ignores latitude and sidereal time
confirmed.evidence: `hourDiffFrom6AMByLocalSol = (hour + minute/60) - 6; ascLong = sunLong + hourDiffFrom6AMByLocalSol*15`. The true ascendant depends on geographic latitude, LST, and obliquity; a flat 15°/hour offset from sunrise can be wrong by 90°+ (whole quadrants of the zodiac). Houses are then `Math.floor(((p.long - ascLong + 360)%360)/30)+1` — equal 30° houses anchored to this wrong ascendant, so every house assignment inherits the error.

confirmed.title: Present-but-empty WuXing renders five fake 0% bars (B-002 fake-zero)
confirmed.evidence: wuxingAvail=Boolean(raw.wuxing); empty wu_xing_vector -> weightTotal 0 -> all elements 0 but available true; WuXingDetail guard only checks !available. Junk test realshapes.test.ts:299 never asserts available/distribution.

confirmed.title: boot failure exits 0, defeats ON_FAILURE
confirmed.evidence: catch no exit(1); throw skips listen, exits 0; ON_FAILURE wont retry

confirmed.title: birth_data stored RAW from req.body, not the sanitized validation.value — garbage/extra keys persist in jsonb
confirmed.evidence: Both handlers call `validateBirthInput(birth_data ?? {})` purely as a gate, then `.insert({ ..., birth_data })` storing the ORIGINAL req.body value. The validator returns a cleaned `value: ValidatedBirthInput` (birthInputValidation.ts:143-158, trims/normalizes/defaults gender), but it is never read. Consequence: a client POST `{ birth_data: { name:'X', birthDate:'2000-01-01', ..., evil:'<script>', huge: '...10MB...', lat: '52.37' } }` passes validation (only known fields are checked, unknown keys are ignored) and the WHOLE object — extra keys, string-typed lat that the validator coerced internally but did not persist — is written to jsonb. This breaks the documented contract: docs/contracts/supabase-schema.md:17 says birth_data is the `ValidatedBirthInput`-shape. No jsonb schema/CHECK constraint exists in the migration, so the DB accepts anything. Downstream reads (GET selects birth_data and feeds it back to validateBirthInput / FuFirE) will re-validate, but the at-rest data is unbounded and uncanonical.

confirmed.title: BaZiDetail hardcodes fabricated '1.5x Wichtung' metric; wording scanners skip detail components
confirmed.evidence: Fixed JSX literal '1.5x Wichtung', not from any engine/viewModel value. Unguarded: identity/metric regex runs only on landing+shell, never detail components.

confirmed.title: Planet table shows 'Haus 0' when house unknown
confirmed.evidence: house:0 means unknown but renders 'Haus 0' (table outside the housesAvailable guard at :134).

confirmed.title: Malformed JSON body returns Express default HTML 500, breaking the {error,message} JSON contract
confirmed.featureArea: API error handling / all POST routes
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:262 (express.json(); no error-handling middleware in entire file)
confirmed.evidence: createApp registers `app.use(express.json())` at app.ts:262 and returns at line 619 with NO 4-arg `(err,req,res,next)` handler anywhere (grep for `app.use((err` / `err, req, res, next` over app.ts returns nothing). When a client posts an invalid JSON body, express.json() throws a SyntaxError that bubbles to Express's default finalhandler, which responds with an HTML 500 page (Content-Type text/html), not the `{error, message}` JSON every other route emits via sendError (app.ts:65-71). The frontend client does `await res.json().catch(() => ({}))` (bazodiacClient.ts:229) so it silently loses the error code, surfacing a generic `http_500` instead of a typed error. Also wrong status class: a client-side bad body should be 400, not 500.

confirmed.title: Profile/partner write routes persist the RAW request body, discarding validator normalization
confirmed.evidence: POST /api/me/profiles runs `validateBirthInput(birth_data ?? {})` (app.ts:540) but the insert writes the untouched `birth_data` request field: `.insert({ user_id, label, birth_data, is_default })` (app.ts:556). It never uses `validation.value`. So the validator's normalization is thrown away: trimmed/normalized name, `timeKnown===false -> birthTime '12:00'` (birthInputValidation.ts:95-96), gender defaulting to 'Divers' (line 155), and parsed numeric lat/lon. Worse, the raw object is stored, so any extra client-supplied keys land verbatim in nb_profiles.birth_data jsonb. Same pattern for partners at app.ts:600. Round-trip drift: what is validated is NOT what is stored or later replayed (AccountMenu.tsx:220 calls onProfileLoad(p.birth_data)).

confirmed.title: POST /api/timezone fabricates an 'Etc/GMT' null-island timezone for null/empty/false coordinates (HTTP 200)
confirmed.evidence: Live: POST /api/timezone -d '{"lat":null,"lon":null}' -> HTTP 200 {"tz":"Etc/GMT","utcOffsetMinutes":0}; identical output for '{"lat":0,"lon":0}'. The guard at app.ts:345 only rejects `lat === undefined || lon === undefined`; null/''/false survive it, then Number(null)=Number('')=Number(false)=0 (app.ts:350) and tzlookup(0,0) returns the valid ocean tz 'Etc/GMT'. Coordinates the user never supplied silently resolve to a real-looking timezone instead of a missing-state error.

confirmed.title: signalLevel ('leise/spürbar/dominant') is emitted while coherenceIndex is null — a strength claim about a value the app admits is missing
confirmed.evidence: coherenceIndex is null unless h_calibrated OR a legacy coherence value exists (lines 611-616). signalLevel is computed independently from calibration.h_raw/h_baseline/h_sigma (lines 629-631). When the engine sends calibration: { h_raw, h_baseline, h_sigma } but NO h_calibrated, coherenceIndex===null yet signalLevel==='spuerbar'. The existing test makes this explicit and GREEN: 'liefert coherenceIndex null …' asserts `coherenceIndex toBeNull()` AND `signalLevel toBe('spuerbar')` simultaneously (test lines 312-314). The product principle forbids fabricated metrics / fake state; presenting a non-null signal-strength bucket ('spürbar') for a coherence the UI shows as 'Keine Kohärenz-Daten verfügbar' (line 642) is an internal contradiction the user sees as a confident reading on top of a 'no data' headline.

confirmed.title: No retry/backoff on transient upstream failures (429 rate-limit, 503, network blip, timeout) — single fetch only
confirmed.evidence: request() performs exactly one fetch with an AbortController timeout (12s default) and maps the result. 429 → fufire_rate_limited (line 127), 503/5xx → fufire_unavailable. There is no retry, no exponential backoff, no honoring of a Retry-After header. The capabilities/health surface and every chart/calculate call thus fail hard on a single transient. Combined with FUFIRE-ORCH-01, one transient blip on any of 4 parallel calls fails the whole profile with zero resilience.

confirmed.title: wuxing section present-but-empty/garbage renders a 0%-everywhere distribution as 'available' instead of missing-state
confirmed.location: src/utils/fufireNormalizer.ts:530-588 (wuxingAvail = isFallback || Boolean(raw.wuxing); distribution defaults to 0 when weightTotal===0)
confirmed.evidence: wuxingAvail is true whenever raw.wuxing is any truthy object (line 530), regardless of whether the vector carries usable weights. If the engine returns wuxing:{} or wuxing:{ wu_xing_vector:{} } or all-non-positive weights, weightTotal===0 (line 550-551) and every element's share becomes 0 (line 557). elementCards are still built (line 565) with status logic `percent<15 → Defizit`, so ALL FIVE elements render as 'Defizit' at 0% — a fabricated 'everything is deficient' reading. The vectorExplanation also interpolates distribution[maxElement] (0%) into a sentence claiming a 'dominante …-Frequenz (0%)'. This is the same class of bug as B-002 (0 instead of missing) but for wuxing, which only the all-zero guard misses. Contrast: fusion correctly returns coherenceIndex null in the analogous case.

confirmed.title: B-005/FR-008 PDF report still unimplemented — 'Bericht herunterladen' is bare window.print()
confirmed.evidence: onClick={() => window.print()} on #print-report-btn labeled 'Bericht herunterladen'. No report template, no PDF engine, no chaptered Western/BaZi/WuXing/Fusion/disclaimer structure anywhere in src. Matches ledger B-005/FR-008 (status: still only browser print).

confirmed.title: AccountMenu a11y gaps + ExplanationLayer no focus-trap/restore + magic-link no aria-live
confirmed.evidence: AccountMenu trigger no aria-expanded/haspopup; panel no role/Escape/outside-close, only X. Magic-link error not shown in link-sent view, no aria-live. ExplanationLayer no focus trap and no focus restore on close.

confirmed.title: VITE_SUPABASE_* build args unwired, no guard
confirmed.evidence: ARGs bake creds; supabase=null if absent kills login; railway.toml no build.args

confirmed.title: DELETE routes always return 204 even when nothing was deleted (no-op indistinguishable from success)
confirmed.evidence: Both DELETE handlers run `.delete().eq('id',id).eq('user_id',userId)` and, absent a Supabase error, unconditionally `res.status(204).send()` (app.ts:572, app.ts:616). They never inspect affected-row count, so deleting a non-existent id, an already-deleted id, or another user's row all return 204. The test app.profiles.test.ts:167-176 explicitly asserts that User B deleting User A's profile returns 204 while the row survives - i.e. the owner filter (security) is correct but the response lies: the caller is told the delete succeeded. A REST client cannot distinguish a real delete from a silent no-op.

confirmed.title: Malformed / non-object JSON body leaks full stack trace and absolute file paths to the client (no error middleware)
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:262 app.use(express.json()) — createApp() registers no error-handling middleware
confirmed.evidence: Live: POST /api/geocode -d '{bad json' and POST /api/azodiac/profile -d '"hello"' both return HTTP 400 with Express's default HTML error page containing: 'SyntaxError: Expected property name... at JSON.parse (<anonymous>) at parse (/Users/benjaminpoersch/Projects/New_Bazi/node_modules/body-parser/lib/types/json.js:92:19) ... at process.processTicksAndRejections'. This bypasses the sendError() helper whose docstring (app.ts:64) states it exists to 'never leak secrets or stack traces to the browser'. No app.use((err,req,res,next)=>...) is registered, so body-parse errors fall through to Express's default handler which serializes err.stack with full repo + node_modules paths.

confirmed.title: POST /api/places/autocomplete with non-string input throws unhandled TypeError -> 500
confirmed.evidence: Live: POST /api/places/autocomplete -d '{"input":12345}' -> HTTP 500 {"error":"internal_error","message":"input.trim is not a function"}. Handler passes req.body?.input straight to getAutocompletePredictions, which at mapsService.ts:138 calls input.trim().length without a typeof guard; a non-string truthy value throws TypeError, surfaced verbatim by sendError().

confirmed.title: POST /api/gemini/reading returns 502 'service unavailable' for a missing/invalid prompt instead of 400, after a wasted upstream call
confirmed.evidence: Live (GEMINI_API_KEY is configured; a valid prompt returns 200 {"text":"Willkommen."}): POST /api/gemini/reading with {}, {"prompt":null}, and {"prompt":{"injected":"..."}} all return HTTP 502 {"error":"gemini_error","message":"Gemini-Deutung ist derzeit nicht verfügbar."}. The handler never validates `prompt` before calling ai.models.generateContent({contents: prompt}); an undefined/null/object prompt makes the SDK throw, which is caught and mapped to a generic 502. A pure client input error is reported as an upstream outage AND still spends a real Gemini API call.

confirmed.title: label is unbounded and un-trimmed (only truthiness checked on partners; profiles accept any string)
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:539 & 587-590; migration :8 (label text default) & :21 (label text not null)
confirmed.evidence: Profiles: `const { label = 'Mein Profil', ... } = req.body` then inserted with no length/trim/type check — a non-string or megabyte-long label is stored. Partners: only `if (!label)` (truthiness) is checked, so an empty-after-trim string ('   ') passes, and there is no max length. Column type is plain `text` with no CHECK. Compare with birthInputValidation which bounds name to 2..80 chars — label gets no equivalent guard. validateBirthInput does NOT cover label.

confirmed.title: /api/timezone returns 502 (server/provider error) for bad client coordinates (NaN / out-of-range) — client error masquerades as server error
confirmed.evidence: The route only guards lat/lon === undefined → 400 missing_coordinates (app.ts:345-347). It then does `getTimezone(Number(lat), Number(lon), ...)` with NO range/NaN check. In mapsService.getTimezone, tzlookup(lat,lon) throws 'invalid coordinates' for NaN or |lat|>90 (verified: tz-lookup v6.1.25 `tz(NaN,NaN)` throws), and the catch maps ANY throw to `PlacesError('places_provider_error')` → httpStatus 502. So a malformed client request (e.g. lat='abc' → NaN, or lat=999) is reported as a 502 'Ortsdienst ist derzeit nicht verfügbar' — a false 'our backend/provider is down' signal. mapsService.test.ts:211-214 even codifies this: `getTimezone(999,999)` is asserted to reject as places_provider_error. By contrast validateBirthInput DOES range-check lat/lon (birthInputValidation.ts:114-123), so the two entry points disagree on the same boundary.

confirmed.title: calculateSynastry six-harmony table keys on 'Ochse', but the engine only ever produces 'Büffel' — Rat/Ox bonus is permanently dead
confirmed.evidence: animHarmonies maps `"Ratte": "Ochse", "Ochse": "Ratte"`, but EARTHLY_BRANCHES[1].animal is "Büffel" (line 31), and the rest of the codebase (baziCompare.ts LIU_HE_PAIRS line 65 uses ['Ratte','Büffel']) standardizes on 'Büffel'. So `animHarmonies[animal1] === animal2` can never be satisfied for the Rat-Ox pair: animal2 is 'Büffel', the map yields 'Ochse'. The +15 Liu-He bonus for that pair is unreachable. calculateSynastry also has NO callers (grep finds only its definition), so it is currently dead code — but a latent correctness bug the moment it is wired in.

confirmed.title: Zi-hour (23:00–23:59) day-pillar rollover not applied — late-night births get the wrong day pillar
confirmed.location: src/utils/astrology.ts:146-167 (day pillar uses calendar day) and 164-167 (hour branch sets Zi for >=23:00)
confirmed.evidence: The hour branch correctly assigns Zi (idx 0) for decimalHour>=23, but the day pillar is computed purely from the Julian Day of the calendar date (jdOffset at lines 147-151) with no forward roll. In traditional BaZi the 'late Zi' hour (23:00–24:00) belongs to the NEXT day's pillar. Verified before the disk filled: dayPillar('2020-07-01','23:30',0) returns Geng-Zi (same as noon), whereas the late-Zi convention rolls the day stem/branch forward by one.

confirmed.title: compareProfiles synastry collapses each chart to one sun-sign element + one day-master element with hardcoded scores (B-009)
confirmed.evidence: BaZi side reads only `bazi.dayMaster.element` (1 of 8 chart elements) and maps any relation to one of four constants {85 same, 78 generating, 52 controlling, 65 neutral}. Western side reads only `western.sunSign`, reduced to a 4-element bucket with constants {82,70,56}. Final score is a flat average. No branches, no aspects, no inter-aspect orbs, no element distribution weighting — yet it emits a single percentage `score`. The richer, correct machinery (computeInterAspects, derivePairAxes, compareBaziPillars) exists but compareProfiles does not use it. Matches ledger B-009 'synastry too simplified'.

confirmed.title: Local WuXing distribution is renormalized by dumping the rounding remainder entirely onto Earth, distorting the Earth element
confirmed.evidence: After per-element percentages are computed with a `Math.max(5, ...)` floor (line 235), the code forces the total to 100 by `distribution[ElementType.EARTH] += diff` (line 241) where diff = 100 - currentSum. The min-5% clamp can inflate the sum well above 100 (five elements each floored at 5%, plus rounding), so a sizeable negative diff is subtracted only from Earth. This can drive Earth below its true share (even below the 5% floor it just enforced) purely as a math artifact, not from chart content.

confirmed.title: Local western aspects use crude mean longitudes, so aspect detection (and its stated 'auf X° Orb genau') is precise math over imprecise inputs
confirmed.evidence: The aspect orb math itself is correct (angleDiff = rawDiff>180 ? 360-rawDiff : rawDiff; orb=|angleDiff-angle|; maxOrb gate). But it operates on the fabricated mean longitudes from ASTRO-MATH-01, then renders `Effektiv auf ${round(orb,1)}° Orb genau` — asserting sub-degree precision on positions that are wrong by many degrees. The honest layer (interAspects.ts) is fine; this legacy local path overstates precision.

confirmed.title: sendError forwards err.message verbatim — non-typed throws leak internal strings to the browser
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:64-71 (sendError); reached e.g. from /api/places/details via mapsService.ts:182
confirmed.evidence: sendError (app.ts:69) returns `message: err && err.message ? String(err.message) : 'Unerwarteter Fehler.'`. Typed FuFirEError/PlacesError carry safe constant messages, but any plain Error thrown inside a route's try block is forwarded verbatim. Example: getPlaceDetails throws `new Error('Missing placeId parameter in details lookup')` (mapsService.ts:182) — not a PlacesError — and would be sent to the client through /api/places/details's catch→sendError (app.ts:339). Stack traces are NOT exposed (only .message), so impact is limited to internal message strings, but the 'never leaking secrets or stack traces' contract on line 64 is weaker than stated for non-typed throws.

confirmed.title: Open unauthenticated outbound proxy to Photon (autocomplete) with no throttle
confirmed.evidence: /api/places/autocomplete is anonymous and unthrottled; it forwards req.body.input to https://photon.komoot.io via fetch (mapsService.ts:147). Input is URL-encoded with encodeURIComponent (mapsService.ts:147), so it cannot break out of the query param (no SSRF to arbitrary hosts — host is hardcoded). Residual risk is using the BFF as a free, unmetered proxy/relay to a third party (OSM etiquette/abuse, and amplification against photon.komoot.io from the app's IP). /api/places/details decodes a self-contained base64url placeId with strict type checks (mapsService.ts:80-92) — no network, safe.

confirmed.title: /api/timezone coerces non-numeric lat/lon to NaN and forwards it to the provider instead of 400
confirmed.evidence: The route only guards `lat === undefined || lon === undefined` (app.ts:345), then calls `getTimezone(Number(lat), Number(lon), ...)` (app.ts:350). For body {lat:'abc', lon:'def'} the undefined check passes, Number('abc') yields NaN, and NaN coordinates are sent to the provider. There is no NaN/range guard (contrast birthInputValidation.ts:112-124 which explicitly rejects NaN and out-of-range lat/lon for birth input). Best case the provider errors and sendError maps it to 502 (a server-fault status for what is bad client input); worst case it silently resolves a wrong/garbage tz. Either way the route should 400.

confirmed.title: Partner routes and both DELETE routes have NO frontend caller; profile delete/manage UI is missing
confirmed.location: src/components/AccountMenu.tsx (only caller of /api/me/*); app.ts:575-617 (partner GET/POST/DELETE, profile DELETE)
confirmed.evidence: AccountMenu.tsx is the sole frontend consumer of the /api/me/* surface and it calls only GET /api/me/profiles (loadProfiles, line 54) and POST /api/me/profiles (saveProfile, line 75). There is no fetch to /api/me/profiles/:id (DELETE), and nothing anywhere touches /api/me/partners (GET/POST/DELETE) - confirmed by grep of src outside src/server for those paths returning only AccountMenu's two profile calls. So 4 of the 6 auth-gated routes (partner CRUD + profile delete) ship with backend + tests but no UI path: a saved profile can be created and loaded but never deleted by a user, and the entire partner-persistence backend is dead from the client's perspective. Synastry takes partner birth data ad hoc, never from /api/me/partners.

confirmed.title: POST /api/geocode with non-string placeId throws unhandled TypeError -> 500 with raw JS error
confirmed.evidence: Live: POST /api/geocode -d '{"placeId":12345}' -> HTTP 500 {"error":"internal_error","message":"placeId.startsWith is not a function"}. The route guard only checks falsiness (`if (!placeId)`), so a truthy non-string (number/array/object) passes through to getPlaceDetails -> decodePlaceId(placeId) which calls placeId.startsWith() at mapsService.ts:81 and throws a TypeError; sendError() then surfaces err.message verbatim.

confirmed.title: Non-atomic clear-then-insert for is_default races the one-default unique index; 23505 surfaces as opaque 502
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:547-560 (POST /api/me/profiles, makeDefault path) + migration :15-16 (nb_profiles_one_default partial unique index)
confirmed.evidence: makeDefault=true runs two separate statements with no transaction: (1) UPDATE nb_profiles SET is_default=false WHERE user_id=… then (2) INSERT … is_default=true. Two concurrent makeDefault:true requests for the same user can both clear, then both insert is_default=true, hitting the partial-unique index `create unique index nb_profiles_one_default on nb_profiles(user_id) where is_default`. The insert error path (:559) maps ANY error to `db_error` / httpStatus 502 with message 'Datenbankfehler.' — there is no 23505/onConflict handling, no retry, no transaction wrapper. The unique index correctly PREVENTS two defaults (good), but the app turns the expected conflict into a generic 502 instead of resolving it. Also the clear-UPDATE is not gated on the new row existing, so a failed insert after a successful clear leaves the user with ZERO defaults.

confirmed.title: No per-user row cap on nb_profiles / nb_partner_profiles — unbounded storage growth per account
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:538-560 (profiles POST), :586-604 (partners POST); migration has no constraint
confirmed.evidence: Neither POST handler counts existing rows before inserting; grep for `.limit`/`MAX_`/count guards in src/server returns nothing. A single authenticated user can create an unbounded number of profiles and partner profiles (each carrying an unbounded jsonb birth_data + unbounded label, see DB-INTEGRITY-04). With service-role writes there is no DB-side quota. This is a denial-of-wallet / storage-abuse boundary hole, and there is no test asserting any cap (src/server/app.profiles.test.ts has no count-limit case).

confirmed.title: nb_partner_profiles has no updated_at column and no touch trigger — edits are untracked and asymmetric with nb_profiles
confirmed.evidence: nb_partner_profiles defines only created_at (:23); there is no updated_at and the touch trigger `nb_profiles_touch` is attached solely to nb_profiles (:42-44). nb_profiles GET orders by updated_at, but partners GET orders by created_at (app.ts:581) — and there is currently no partner UPDATE route, so any future edit-in-place would be silently untracked. This is a documented-but-accepted asymmetry (schema doc :22-31 lists no updated_at for partners), but it is a latent integrity gap: once partner editing ships, there is no last-modified signal and no way to sort by recency-of-edit.

confirmed.title: No lower year bound — 0000/0001 and pre-1582 (pre-Gregorian) dates accepted and forwarded to the engine
confirmed.evidence: The only date checks are DATE_RE (^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$), an ISO round-trip, and a future-date guard. Reproduced the exact logic: '0000-01-01' → ACCEPTED, '0001-01-01' → ACCEPTED, '1000-06-15' → ACCEPTED, '1581-10-05' → ACCEPTED. There is no minimum-year floor. JS Date treats all proleptic-Gregorian, but real astronomical/ephemeris engines (and Gregorian-vs-Julian calendar reform on 1582-10-15) make pre-modern dates either undefined or silently wrong. These dates flow unmodified into localIsoDatetime() (fufirePayloadMappers.ts:111-112) and into every calculate/experience payload. A typo like '0190-06-15' (missing a digit) is indistinguishable from a real date to the validator and yields a garbage chart presented with the same confidence as a valid one.

confirmed.title: /api/geocode resolves tz via tz-lookup at 'now', and tz-lookup uses current political boundaries — historical births near moved-borders/zone-splits get the wrong tz_id
confirmed.location: src/server/app.ts:357-379 (getTimezone(det.lat, det.lon) with no timestamp); src/utils/mapsService.ts:213-217
confirmed.evidence: /api/geocode calls getTimezone(det.lat, det.lon) WITHOUT a timestamp, so atMs defaults to Date.now() (mapsService.ts:214). The route comment (app.ts:366-368) correctly notes it deliberately omits utcOffsetMinutes because offset is birth-instant dependent — good — but the returned tz_id itself comes from tz-lookup, which maps coordinates to the CURRENT IANA zone for that point. For a location whose IANA zone was split/renamed/reassigned after the birth (e.g. zones that diverged mid-20th-century, or a place that changed its governing zone), the tz_id stored against the profile reflects today's politics, not the birth-era zone. Downstream the engine applies DST/offset for that tz_id at the birth date, which can be off by a full hour for affected locations. This is a known hard limit of coordinate→tz lookup but is undisclosed; the app presents the resolved tz as authoritative.

confirmed.title: No geocoding provider fallback — single Photon dependency means any Photon outage hard-fails all place search; the keyless rewrite removed redundancy
confirmed.location: src/utils/mapsService.ts:65,137-173 (single PHOTON_URL, no fallback); branch fix/free-geocoding-providers exists
confirmed.evidence: getAutocompletePredictions has exactly one provider: PHOTON_URL = 'https://photon.komoot.io/api/' (line 65). On any non-ok response or fetch throw it maps to PlacesError('places_provider_error') (502) (168-172) with NO secondary provider attempt. getPlaceDetails likewise only handles self-contained pl1: ids and demo ids — a legacy Google placeId or any non-pl1 id is an immediate 502 (193-195). The comment block (55-63) frames Photon as THE provider. A `fix/free-geocoding-providers` branch exists (git branch -a), implying multi-provider work is in flight but unmerged on main. So in production today, a single third-party (komoot) outage or rate-limit takes down all birth-place entry — the very first step of every flow — with no degraded path. OSM etiquette (single shared User-Agent 'New_Bazi/1.0', line 66) also risks shared rate-limiting.

confirmed.title: Orchestrated profile cannot distinguish 'all 4 sections merged' from 'a section silently failed' — source label and provenance both over-claim
confirmed.evidence: Because FUFIRE-ORCH-01 makes Promise.all all-or-nothing today, the codebase has no path where a section is missing post-orchestration via partial success. But the moment ORCH-01 is fixed with allSettled (the correct fix), there is no carrier for per-section upstream-failure provenance: the normalizer infers status purely from Boolean(raw.western|bazi|wuxing|fusion) (lines 738/746/753/761), which cannot tell 'engine never asked' from 'engine asked and the call failed'. A failed section would be labelled identically to a never-present one ('Nicht von FuFirE geliefert (missing)'), and source stays 'fufire-orchestrated'. The provenance matrix is honesty-critical surface; it should record an explicit 'upstream-error' status distinct from 'missing'. Filed as a gap that blocks the correct ORCH-01 fix from being fully honest.

confirmed.title: Ledger marks Synastry/FR-011 as 'nicht vorhanden / nur vereinfachter Score' — code already implements the full pair journey
confirmed.evidence: Ledger §6 B-009 + §7 FR-011 + table row 19/3 claim synastry is only score=avg(baziScore,westernScore) and 'Aspekte/BaZi-Polachsen aktuell nicht vorhanden'. Code contradicts this: /api/azodiac/synastry returns interAspects (computeInterAspects, real orb math, luminary 8°/default 6° tolerance), pillarComparison (compareBaziPillars: San-He/Liu-He/Chong + Sheng/Ke stem relations), pairAxes (derivePairAxes), elementalA/B mirror. PartnerJourney.tsx renders all four layers (data-testid synastry-axes/aspects/pillars/elements) with data anchors + honest empty states. The naive score survives ONLY as a labeled 'grober Vergleich' badge with a 'kein Messwert' note (Synastry.tsx:236,248). FR-011 is ~DONE, not missing.

confirmed.title: nb_partner_profiles lacks updated_at + touch trigger that nb_profiles has — schema asymmetry vs FR-001 'partner_profiles' intent
confirmed.evidence: nb_profiles has updated_at + nb_profiles_touch trigger (lines 11-12,42-44) and the GET /api/me/profiles orders by updated_at (app.ts:533). nb_partner_profiles (lines 18-24) has NO updated_at column and NO touch trigger; GET /api/me/partners orders by created_at (app.ts:581). Partner rows are insert-only (no PUT/PATCH route exists), so editing a partner means delete+recreate. FR-001 lists 'partner_profiles' as a first-class persistence table; the asymmetry is an under-modeled gap, not a bug today.

confirmed.title: Ledger 'Validierung'/Bug-Report over-states open work: B-001, B-002, B-006, B-004/FR-015 are DONE in code but listed as open/missing
confirmed.location: new-bazi-full-feature-validation-and-sprint-plan-v2.md §4 rows 5/12/17/24/26, §6 B-001/B-002/B-006; vs src/components/WuXingDetail.tsx:27-589, src/utils/fufireNormalizer.ts:611-642, src/components/Overview.tsx:7-474, src/utils/birthInputValidation.ts:16-102
confirmed.evidence: B-001 coaching: WuXingDetail now uses reflectionText + label 'Alltagsimpuls' (no 'Coaching-Vektor' in user text). B-002 0%-coherence: fufireNormalizer sets coherenceIndex=null with coherenceCalibrated/signalLevel and 'Keine Kohärenz-Daten verfügbar' (no fake 0%). B-006/FR-007 Overview layers: ExplanationLayer + content/registry with clickable sun/moon/asc/pillar/element doors exist. B-004/FR-015 unknown-time: timeKnown threads through validation (birthInputValidation.ts:93-102), normalizer provisional_fields short-circuit asc/houses/hour, and Overview shows honest 'Zeit unbekannt' absence. The ledger (dated 2026-06-11) predates these merges and still lists them as open — anyone planning from it would re-do finished work.

confirmed.title: Write routes persist RAW request birth_data (and unbounded label) instead of validated value
confirmed.evidence: app.ts:540 validates: `const validation = validateBirthInput(birth_data ?? {})` but app.ts:556 then inserts the ORIGINAL untrusted body: `.insert({ user_id: userId, label, birth_data, is_default: !!makeDefault })`. The normalized object `validation.value` (trimmed name/tz, coerced lat/lon, dropped extra keys — birthInputValidation.ts:143-158) is discarded. validateBirthInput ignores unknown keys, so a client can attach arbitrary extra fields / deeply nested JSON which are stored verbatim in the jsonb column (bounded only by express.json's 100kb default). `label` is taken raw from req.body with no max-length on either route (partners route has no label length check at all, app.ts:587-589).

confirmed.title: env drift: GOOGLE_MAPS dead, APP_URL unused, DISABLE_HMR undoc
confirmed.evidence: GOOGLE_MAPS test-only; APP_URL unread; DISABLE_HMR undocumented

confirmed.title: DELETE of the default profile leaves the user with zero defaults (no reassignment, no warning)
confirmed.evidence: DELETE issues `.delete().eq('id',…).eq('user_id',…)` and returns 204. There is no check for whether the deleted row was is_default=true and no logic to promote another profile to default. After deleting the default, the user has remaining profiles but none flagged default; the partial-unique index permits zero defaults (it only forbids two), so the DB is silently in a 'no default' state. UI code that assumes 'exactly one default exists' (docs/contracts/supabase-schema.md:10 'max. eines ist is_default') can now read zero. No test covers default-deletion (app.profiles.test.ts DELETE cases use non-default rows).

confirmed.title: Self-contained pl1: placeId carries client-trusted lat/lon with no range validation in getPlaceDetails → bypasses geocode integrity
confirmed.evidence: decodePlaceId only checks that lat/lon are `typeof === 'number'` (line 85) — it does NOT range-check them. getPlaceDetails returns `{ lat: decoded.lat, lon: decoded.lon }` verbatim (191). /api/geocode then calls getTimezone(det.lat, det.lon) (app.ts:365). Because pl1: ids are base64url JSON minted client-side-roundtrippable, a crafted/garbled placeId like pl1:<base64 of {n,a,lat:91,lon:200}> resolves to out-of-range coordinates that then throw inside tz-lookup and surface as a 502 (same masking as GEO-TZ-04), or — for in-range-but-wrong values (e.g. swapped lat/lon: a Berlin user encoded as lat:13.4, lon:52.5) — silently resolve to a wrong timezone/location with no detic check. The integer fields are validated for type but never for plausibility, so the 'no default location ever / server-side delegation only' promise (app.ts:356) is only as strong as the opaque-looking but client-forgeable placeId.

confirmed.title: DST-gap 422 is only decoded for /chart; the /v1/calculate/* mappers send nonexistentTime:'error', but the dst_error discriminator handling is not exercised for those endpoints
confirmed.location: src/utils/fufireClient.ts:178-189 (dst detection in shared request) + fufirePayloadMappers.ts:140/156/166/178 (nonexistentTime:'error')
confirmed.evidence: The shared request() decodes body.type==='dst_error' on any 422/400 (line 185), which is good — it applies to all endpoints. BUT the only fixture/test for dst_error is chart-422-dst.json against postChart (fufireClient.test.ts:190-198). The western/bazi/wuxing/fusion mappers all hardcode nonexistentTime:'error' (so the engine WILL 422 on a DST-gap birth time for those endpoints too), yet there is no test asserting that a /v1/calculate/* 422 with type:'dst_error' yields invalid_birth_time_dst rather than a generic invalid_fufire_payload. If the engine's /v1 422 body shape differs (e.g. type under detail[].type instead of top-level body.type), the user-facing 'choose a time before 02:00' guidance silently degrades to the generic 'FuFirE rejected the data' for the orchestrated path. Untested contract assumption on the higher-traffic endpoints.

confirmed.title: getFuFirePathPrefix silently coerces an unknown FUFIRE_API_VERSION to 'v1', masking a real version/prefix misconfig as a 404
confirmed.evidence: getFuFirePathPrefix: raw = explicit || (legacy==='v1'||'/v1' ? legacy : 'v1'). So if FUFIRE_API_VERSION is set to a *release label* like '1.0.0-rc1-20260220' (which getFuFireReleaseVersion at line 106-112 explicitly treats as a release, returning it for the version endpoint) AND no FUFIRE_API_PATH_PREFIX is set, the path prefix silently falls back to '/v1'. That is the documented intent (tested at fufireClient.test 'keeps release labels out of upstream routes'), but it also means an operator who *intended* a different route prefix and mistakenly put it in FUFIRE_API_VERSION gets a silent '/v1' and then 404s (fufire_route_not_found) with no config diagnostic distinguishing 'wrong prefix env' from 'engine route genuinely gone'. The principle 'what doesn't work should fail visibly' favors a startup log when FUFIRE_API_VERSION is non-empty and non-v1 while FUFIRE_API_PATH_PREFIX is unset, clarifying that the version is treated as release metadata and the route prefix defaulted to v1.

refuted.title: Gemini reading route hardcodes a non-existent model id 'gemini-3.5-flash' and has zero test coverage
refuted.reason: The finding's central claim — that 'gemini-3.5-flash' is a non-existent model id that guarantees failure on every configured call — is factually wrong as of the project's actual timeframe (June 2026). Three independent authoritative sources contradict it: (1) the installed SDK @google/genai@2.6.0 (satisfies the package.json constraint ^2.4.0) explicitly enumerates 'gemini-3.5-flash' in its Model_2 TypeScript union type in node_modules/@google/genai/dist/node/node.d.ts, alongside other Gemini 3.x ids (gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.1-*); the SDK the code actually uses treats this id as valid. (2) Official Google docs (ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/interactions/whats-new-gemini-3.5) state Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available, stable, and production-ready, doc-updated 2026-06-04. (3) Web search confirms GA release on 2026-05-19. The environment date is 2026-06-15, well after release. The finding author reasoned from a pre-2026 knowledge cutoff (when valid flash ids were only 1.5/2.0/2.5) and did not verify against current model availability or the installed SDK. The code at app.ts:509 does use this model id (that part of the report is accurate), and the .env file does contain a real GEMINI_API_KEY making the configured path reachable — but that path would SUCCEED, not fail. The secondary 'zero test coverage' sub-claim is literally true (rg -li gemini over test files = 0 hits; no test exercises the configured-success or error branch), but the finding frames this as evidence that a defect 'was never caught' — there is no defect to catch, so the missing tests masked nothing. Untested optional Gemini endpoint is at most a minor hygiene gap, not the medium/medium bug described. The finding as written (invalid model id, guaranteed failure, masked outage) is refuted.

refuted.title: /api/places/autocomplete forwards empty/missing input upstream; no min-length guard (asymmetric with client)
refuted.reason: The finding's central, explicitly-stated claim is false. It asserts that `{}` or `{input:''}` "issues a real upstream Photon query for an empty string" and that "Empty string [is] forwarded to the Photon provider on every empty/missing-input request." It is not. The handler at src/server/app.ts:323 does `getAutocompletePredictions(req.body?.input || "")`, so both `{}` and `{input:''}` yield `""`. But `getAutocompletePredictions` (src/utils/mapsService.ts:137-140) opens with its own guard: `if (!input || input.trim().length === 0) return [];` — this short-circuits and returns `[]` BEFORE the Photon `fetch` at line 148. The author reasoned only from app.ts and the client guard (bazodiacClient.ts:258) and missed the server-side util guard. This is independently confirmed by an existing PASSING test, src/utils/mapsService.test.ts:75-80, which asserts `getAutocompletePredictions("")` resolves to `[]`. So the empty/missing-input contract IS enforced server-side, with no upstream call — directly contradicting the finding's "actual" and "evidence." A residual, much narrower truth exists: the server guard is `length === 0` while the client guard is `< 2`, so a single non-whitespace char (e.g. "a") passes the server but is blocked client-side, meaning a direct caller could trigger a 1-char Photon query. However that 1-char asymmetry is NOT what the finding describes (its title/expected/actual/evidence all center on empty/missing input, which is provably handled), the provider is keyless/free with no stated quota cost, and Photon handles short queries fine. The finding as written is contradicted by the code and an existing test.

refuted.title: Unresolvable / legacy / hostile placeId string returns 502 (provider down) rather than 400 (bad input)
refuted.reason: The finding's factual reproduction is fully accurate: a present-but-unrecognizable placeId (non-'pl1:', legacy Google id, or garbage) does reach getPlaceDetails (mapsService.ts:189-195), where decodePlaceId returns null (line 81) and a PlacesError('places_provider_error') is thrown, mapping to HTTP 502 with message "Ortsdienst ist derzeit nicht verfuegbar." (mapsService.ts:32/36, sendError app.ts:65-70). The /api/geocode route (app.ts:357-380) only returns 400 for a MISSING placeId. The finding also correctly notes this is NOT SQL injection. However, the "contract-violation" conclusion does not hold and is contradicted by the codebase: (1) it is a deliberate, documented choice — mapsService.ts:175-178 explicitly comments that legacy Google place-ids cannot be resolved without Google and are surfaced as an "honest places_provider_error (502)"; (2) the exact behavior is codified by THREE passing tests run under production mode — mapsService.test.ts:166-174 ("rejects legacy Google place-ids with places_provider_error (502)"), mapsService.test.ts:176-180 (corrupted pl1: payloads), and app.test.ts:489-494 ("maps PlacesError for unresolvable legacy placeIds to 502") — which I ran and confirmed green; (3) no published/internal API contract promises a 400 invalid_place_id code, so 502 violates no documented contract. The finding's "expected 400 invalid_place_id" is a defensible REST-hygiene opinion, but flagging behavior that an existing test deliberately asserts as a contract violation is a false positive. At most this is a minor cosmetic API-semantics nicety, not a defect.

refuted.title: DST spring-forward gap times pass validation but hit nonexistentTime:'error' → unrecoverable upstream failure
refuted.reason: The finding's cited code facts are accurate (all four /v1/calculate/* mappers hard-code nonexistentTime:'error' at fufirePayloadMappers.ts:139/154/166/177; TIME_RE at birthInputValidation.ts:99 accepts any HH:mm, so a DST-gap time passes validation; chart-422-dst.json fixture exists). BUT the central thesis — that this yields "a hard 422/error the user cannot self-correct" / "unrecoverable upstream failure" — is contradicted by the code. fufireClient.ts:185-186, inside the GENERIC request() function (so it covers western/bazi/wuxing/fusion, not just /chart), detects `body?.type === "dst_error"` on any 422/400 and maps it to FuFirEError('invalid_birth_time_dst'). That code is configured with httpStatus 400 (ERROR_HTTP_STATUS line 46 — a client-correctable status, NOT a 5xx) and the actionable German message (SAFE_MESSAGES line 57): "Diese Uhrzeit existiert am Umstellungstag nicht (Sommerzeit). Bitte eine Zeit vor 02:00 oder nach 03:00 wählen." detailHandler (app.ts:250-251) catches the error and sendError (app.ts:66-69) forwards that 400 + message to the browser. Tests fufireClient.test.ts:190-198 lock this in. The finding's own `expected` field demands the gap be "caught at validation with an actionable message OR use shift_forward" — option A is met: the engine flags the gap, the client translates it into a 400 with corrective instructions, and the user just re-enters a time outside the gap (fully self-correctable). The "user cannot self-correct" / "unrecoverable" framing is false. The only residual true sub-point is the hard-coded ambiguousTime:'earlier' on fall-back overlaps with no UI disclosure — a real but minor UX nuance (~1h, edge-affecting ascendant/houses), far below the claimed high severity and not the defect the finding centers on.

refuted.title: B-003 daily-sameness root cause persists: quiz_sectors mirror soulprint_sectors (no real per-user daily vector)
refuted.reason:
The literal code observations are all accurate, but the finding's central causal claim — that "quiz_sectors mirror soulprint_sectors" is the persisting ROOT CAUSE of B-003 daily-sameness — is refuted by the project's own live-verified analysis.

Verified facts (all true): fufirePayloadMappers.ts:205-217 sets quiz_sectors = soulprintSectors (date-invariant); the comment at lines 199-203 states the app has no quiz feature; target_date is passed (line 214); the client (DailyPulse.tsx:26,51-55) navigates ±7 days with per-date cache (cacheKey birthKey|targetDate, line 116) and honest "Vorschau/Rückblick" labels; New_Bazi sends no daily_elemental_comparison / Tagesvektor in the daily payload.

Why the finding is wrong on root cause: docs/plans/2026-06-11-sprint-p2-engine-track.md §0 (lines 14-38) records a direct live probe against api.fufire.space/v1/experience/daily with identical birth+sectors across 3 dates. Result: eastern.summary IS different per date (day-pillar + daily variants work); only western.summary/caution/themes are byte-identical, caused by transit.py slow-planet weighting (PLANET_WEIGHTS: Pluto 2.0, Moon 0.5) — an ENGINE-side granularity problem, not the sector arrays. Lines 31-38 explicitly explain a meaningful daily vector cannot be derived from sectors at all; it must come from transit planets + the sexagesimal day-pillar. Therefore quiz_sectors == soulprint_sectors is orthogonal to daily variation — those are the user's static identity ring, by design, and editing them would not change any per-date output.

The cited location is thus a misattribution: the described fix (per B-003 acceptance and FR-012 — date-sensitive Western + daily_elemental_comparison) is routed to the FuFirE engine repo (MASTER roadmap P2/P9), not to fufirePayloadMappers.ts. The mirroring is documented, intentional, and pinned by an existing test (fufirePayloadMappers.test.ts:138-157) as the "only honest sector signal" — not a latent defect.

The one genuinely-valid kernel — there is no New_Bazi client-side MISSING surface when the engine returns identical content across dates — is a minor optional enhancement, not the gap as framed, and the real per-date variation already flows correctly via target_date through eastern + (per engine fix) western.

refuted.title: FR-014 Signatur/Cymatics + FR-013 Rat der 6 + FR-002/003 Quizzes + FR-004/005/006 Voice are entirely MISSING (ledger-consistent, but the 'Signatur geladen' banner overstates state)
refuted.reason:
Two-part finding. Part 1 (FR-002/003/004/005/006/013/014/017 entirely missing) is accurate but is explicitly a non-defect — the finding itself says these features are "correctly absent" open feature requests. I confirmed absence: no cymatics/SignatureState model, no three.js or 3D deps in package.json, no quiz registry/scoring, no Levi/Eve voice endpoints or sphere, no Rat-der-6 module, no Space-Weather endpoint. Consistent with ledger lines 110/140/325-389/496-524/800.

Part 2 — the only thing presented as an actual problem, the "Signatur geladen" banner (Overview.tsx:166) — is refuted. The claim is that the label "pre-announces a not-yet-built capability" (FR-014 3D Cymatics Sphere), violating anti-reification. Evidence against:
(1) "Signatur" already has an established, currently-SHIPPED meaning in the product: the computed West x BaZi x WuXing fusion profile. It appears in 5+ live landing/product strings (LandingPage.tsx:48, engineBento.ts:27, FusionHero.tsx:107 "zu einer berechneten Signatur", trustContent.ts:66, FusionPathSection.tsx:16). The ledger itself (line 510) frames FR-014 as a *reactivation/visualization* ("wird als eigenstaendiges Feature reaktiviert") of that already-named concept via a 3D sphere — not the coining of the term.
(2) The banner is gated: App.tsx:116 (if (!viewModel || !birthData) return early) means Overview only renders when a real compiled fusion viewModel exists. Loading has its own branch (line 73); missing data returns before the Overview case (line 137). So when "Signatur geladen" shows, a computed signature genuinely HAS been loaded and is displayed directly below it (name, birth data, Tagesmeister). The label is literally accurate, not a fabricated/empty state.
(3) The dedicated wording-honesty tests (redesignWording.test.ts:18) define the forbidden identity-claim corpus (du bist/garantiert/beweist/wahres ich/etc.) and do NOT flag "Signatur geladen". It makes no identity claim, no fabricated metric, no guarantee — it is a neutral status pill, not an anti-reification violation.

The accurate component is a non-defect by the finding's own framing; the sole component framed as a defect does not hold up against the established product vocabulary, the render gating, and the honesty test suite.

refuted.title: docs/reality/*.evidence.jsonl could not be audited this session — could not enumerate or read any evidence-ledger file
refuted.reason:
The finding is a self-reported prior-session tooling gap, not a code defect, and its core factual premise does not hold in the current environment. CLAIM: no docs/reality/*.evidence.jsonl could be enumerated or read (guessed names RD-6/P4/reality-ledger all "File does not exist"). ACTUAL: the directory contains exactly two readable evidence files — /Users/benjaminpoersch/Projects/New_Bazi/docs/reality/bazodiac-redesign.evidence.jsonl (10 entries) and .../bazi-sprint-p7-partner-journey.evidence.jsonl (12 entries) — both ~4KB, read cleanly. The guessed filenames were simply wrong; the real ones are feature-named.

I then performed the exact cross-check the finding said was uncovered ("flag any ledger 'verified' claim contradicted by code"). Result: clean bill. Every test artifact referenced by every "verified" entry exists on disk (polar.test.ts, tensionFieldVisual.test.ts, microExperience.test.ts, engineBento.test.ts, redesignWording.test.ts, interAspects.test.ts, baziCompare.test.ts, tensionPair.test.ts, fufireNormalizer.realshapes.test.ts, synastryWording.test.ts, landing-redesign.spec.ts, synastry-completion.spec.ts). Spot-checked claimed test counts match: polar=7, tensionFieldVisual=5, microExperience=3, engineBento=3, interAspects=18. The one apparent mismatch (baziCompare ledger says 35; only 15 source it() lines) resolves in the ledger's favor — it uses it.each(triads)/it.each(pairs)/it.each(...) parameterized tests at lines 105/120/135 that expand to the runtime count of 35, so the ledger reports the correct runtime figure. No "verified" claim is contradicted by code.

The environment blocker the finding cites (ENOSPC on /private/tmp, no Glob/Grep) does not reproduce: mktemp succeeds, Bash/find work, disk has ~7.2Gi available. The only genuinely real sub-claim — the live-smoke automation blocker — is already disclosed inside the ledgers themselves (e.g. REQ-O-001 notes "Interactive prod synastry render not yet observed (browser automation blocked)"), so it is surfaced, not hidden. Net: no code/product defect, the requested audit is now complete and passes, and the central premise (unreadable ledgers) is false here. Correctly self-classified as type=gap/severity=low but it is not an actionable issue in the current codebase.

refuted.title: healthcheck always 200 even when FuFirE down
refuted.reason: The finding mischaracterizes intentional, documented design as a bug. The code is real and behaves as described (railway.toml:7 points the healthcheck at /api/health; app.ts:299-302 returns 200 unconditionally while embedding the real fufire probe result; app.ts:304-307 /api/fufire/health returns 503 when down). But this is a correct liveness-vs-dependency-health split, not a defect. (1) FuFirE is an external third-party astrology engine. Coupling the container healthcheck to it would be actively harmful: with restartPolicyMaxRetries=3, Railway would restart and then fail the entire deploy on an upstream outage that a restart cannot fix, taking down the SPA, Supabase auth, profile management, and places/geocoding — all of which the BFF serves WITHOUT FuFirE (server.ts serves static dist + /api/places via Photon/tz-lookup + /api/me via Supabase). (2) /api/health does NOT hide the down state: line 301 returns {status:'ok', app:'ok', fufire, time} where `fufire` carries the real probe value ('error' when down), preserving observability and refuting any anti-reification/honesty angle. (3) The separation is explicitly documented as a deliberate decision: docs/plans/2026-05-26-fufire-backend-integration.md:169 lists 'GET /api/health → nur App ok, FuFirE nicht geprueft → App Health + FuFirE Health getrennt', and docs/deploy/google-cloud-run.md:111 says '/api/health returns app + FuFirE health — useful as a startup/liveness signal'. (4) No bug-ledger entry (B-001..B-014 contain nothing about healthchecks), and the existing test app.test.ts:449 asserts the current contract as correct. At most this is an optional enhancement (a separate readiness probe), not a high-severity bug.

refuted.title: no env boot guard; boots operational with all missing
refuted.reason: The finding's code descriptions are factually correct but it mislabels intentional, documented, tested design as a defect. Verified all three citations: server.ts:29-31 guards only dist/index.html (fs.existsSync, throws if missing); supabase.ts:13-21 lazily resolves the client and returns null when SUPABASE_URL/SERVICE_ROLE_KEY are absent; fufireClient.ts:81-95 resolves FUFIRE_API_URL/KEY lazily and throws a typed FuFirEError only at call time. /api/config (app.ts:266-297) indeed returns status:"operational" with baseUrlConfigured:false when unconfigured. However, this is the explicit architecture, not a bug: (1) supabase.ts header comment documents it — "Fehlt die Konfiguration, laeuft New_Bazi vollstaendig anonym weiter (Persistenz-Features zeigen ehrlichen Disabled-State)"; (2) requireUserAuth.ts:16-19 guards every protected route, returning a clean 503 PERSISTENCE_DISABLED — no crash; (3) FuFirE-dependent routes throw FuFirEError caught by sendError → clean 503 missing_fufire_url/key (tested at app.test.ts:108-115) — no crash, no fabricated data; (4) this directly implements the product's enforced ANTI-REIFICATION/HONESTY principle: boot and honestly report missing-state rather than fail-fast or fabricate. No ledger entry (B-001..B-014), FR, contradiction doc, or test anywhere mandates fail-fast/boot-validation on missing env — and the finding itself provides no expected/actual and rates it low/low. Booting operational with honest missing-state is the desired behavior; a fail-fast boot guard would arguably contradict the documented design. No reproducible defect, no user impact, no honesty violation.
